title: Boerington with Mushroom Herb Sauce
excerpt: Ingredients Boerie wrapper 500 g good boerewors, casings removed a little olive oil 125 ml finely chopped onion 250 g finely grated brown mushrooms 4 heaped tablespoons mushroom soup powder 90 ml beef stock finely chopped garlic to taste 65 ml butter 65 ml finely chopped parsley 10 – 12 strips smoked beef (if you can't get it at your supermarket …

seo.title: Boerington with Mushroom Herb Sauce - Marinda Kook

html:
Ingredients

Boerie wrapper

  • 500 g good boerewors, casings removed
  • a little olive oil
  • 125 ml finely chopped onion
  • 250 g finely grated brown mushrooms
  • 4 heaped tablespoons mushroom soup powder
  • 90 ml beef stock
  • finely chopped garlic to taste
  • 65 ml butter
  • 65 ml finely chopped parsley
  • 10 – 12 strips smoked beef (if you can't get it at your supermarket, use streaky bacon)

Beef fillet

  • salt and coarse pepper to taste
  • 1 whole piece of beef fillet – about 800 g – silverskin and sinew removed
  • soft butter
  • a little olive oil
  • 8 – 10 large strips smoked beef or springbok ("bacon" will also do)

Whole Mushrooms

  • 8 extra-large, whole brown mushrooms
  • good beef extract powder (Ina Paarman)
  • olive oil
  • butter

Sauce

  • 30 ml olive oil
  • 250 g grated brown mushrooms (also use the stems from the large mushrooms)
  • 750 ml good red wine
  • 30 ml tomato paste
  • 15 ml finely chopped thyme
  • 15 ml finely chopped rosemary
  • 5 – 10 ml coarsely ground black pepper
  • 1 packet beef soup powder (or oxtail soup powder)
  • 250 ml water
  • 30 ml finely chopped garlic chives
  • 45 ml finely chopped parsley
  • 8 – 16 rocket leaves

Method

Wrapper

Put the sausage in a food processor and chop the meat quite a bit finer.

Heat a little olive oil in a pan and fry the onions lightly.

Add the boerewors mince and stir-fry until the meat is light brown and crumbly.

Add the mushrooms and stir-fry until they become saucy.

Mix the mushroom soup powder with the beef stock and add to the meat mixture.

Cook until the mixture is nicely thick while stirring regularly.

Remove from heat and season to taste with garlic and parsley.

Line a baking tray with baking paper and arrange the strips of smoked beef on top. Make sure they overlap slightly.

Now carefully spread the meat mixture over the smoked beef.

Place in the fridge to cool.

Beef fillet

While the wrapper cools, brown the fillet.

Season it generously with salt and pepper and rub it all over with soft butter.

Heat a little olive oil in a pan (this prevents the butter from burning) and brown the fillet all over.

Take the fillet out of the pan and set aside, loosely covered with foil, to rest.

Now place the fillet along the long side of the meat mixture and, with the help of the baking paper, roll the wrapper, smoked beef strips and all, right around the fillet.

Carefully wrap in foil and bake at 190 ºC for 18 – 25 minutes, or until done to your liking.

Here's a guide if you have a meat thermometer:
Medium-rare: 55 – 56 ºC
Medium: 60 – 62 ºC
Medium-well: 65 – 68 ºC
When the meat is done to your liking, open the foil and let the meat rest for 10 minutes.

Slice carefully and serve with the whole mushrooms and sauce.

Whole Mushrooms

Sprinkle the whole mushrooms with beef extract powder and cook in a mixture of oil and butter in a hot pan until done.

Remove and keep warm to one side.

Sauce

Stir-fry the grated mushrooms in the oil in a hot pan until saucy.

Add the wine, tomato paste, thyme, rosemary and pepper and stir well.

Let it boil rapidly, uncovered, until the mixture has reduced by about a third.

Mix the beef soup powder with the water until smooth and stir into the sauce. Keep stirring until the sauce has
thickened.

Remove from heat and stir in the chives and parsley.

Serving

Serve slices of meat with whole mushrooms on top, drizzled with a generous portion of sauce.

Serves 4 hearty portions.

A lot of work, but EASY!
